{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11383282]]} This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Sado Province''' or the list of shrines in [[Sado Province]] which is present in the {ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}
On this page we will also cover the {ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect} of [[Sado Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the Engishiki list. They are shown in a separate table to avoid confusion.
The ''Engishiki'' counts **9 shikinaisha** in the province – **9 shosha** and **0 taisha**.

On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[Sado Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the Engishiki list. They are shown in a separate table to avoid confusion.
The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} counts 9 {{ill|Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the province – 9 {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} and 0 {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|WD=Q134917288}}.

The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} counts 9 {{ill|Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the province – 9 {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} and 0 {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|WD=Q134917288}}.

On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[Sado Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the Engishiki list. They are shown in a separate table to avoid confusion.
The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} counts 9 {{ill|Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the province – 9 {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} and 0 {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|WD=Q134917288}}.
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! Global !! Prov !! District !! Name !! Kana !! Rank !! Shrine DB !! Celebration !! Seats !! Subtype !! Parent shrine !! [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 4 July 2025 (UTC)}} This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Sado Province''' or the list of shrines in [[Sado Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}
On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[Sado Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the Engishiki list. They are shown in a separate table to avoid confusion.
The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} counts 9 {{ill|Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the province – 9 {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} and 0 {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|WD=Q134917288}}.
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! Global !! Prov !! District !! Name !! Kana !! Rank !! Shrine DB !! Celebration !! Seats !! Subtype !! Parent shrine !! [[:ja:佐渡国の式内社一覧]]
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 14:30, 4 July 2025 (UTC)}} This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Sado Province''' or the list of shrines in [[Sado Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}
On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[Sado Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the Engishiki list. They are shown in a separate table to avoid confusion.
The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} counts 9 {{ill|Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the province – 9 {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} and 0 {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|WD=Q134917288}}.
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! Global !! Prov !! District !! Name !! Kana !! Rank !! Shrine DB !! Celebration !! Seats !! Subtype !! Parent shrine !! Same as |- | 1923 | 1 | Hamochi district | {{ill|Watatsu Shrine|WD=Q172382}} | わたつじんじゃ | Shikinai Shosha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182473 archive] | — | single | — | — | — |- | 1924 | 2 | Hamochi district | {{ill|Ohomeno shrine|WD=Q135040480}} | オホメノ | Shikinai Shosha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182474 archive] | — | single | — | — | — |- | 1925 | 3 | Sawata district | {{ill|Hikitaheno shrine|WD=Q135040483}} | ヒキタヘノ | Shikinai Shosha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182475 archive] | — | single | — | — | — |- | 1926 | 4 | Sawata district | {{ill|Mononoheno shrine|WD=Q135040484}} | モノノヘノ | Shikinai Shosha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182476 archive] | — | single | — | — | — |- | 1927 | 5 | Sawata district | {{ill|Mikeno shrine|WD=Q135040485}} | ミケノ | Shikinai Shosha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182477 archive] | — | single | — | — | {{ill|Mike Shrine|WD=Q135070009}}; {{ill|Daizen Shrine|WD=Q135070010}}; {{ill|御食神社|WD=Q135195179}}; {{ill|大膳神社|WD=Q135195180}} |- | 1928 | 6 | Sawata district | {{ill|Ihimochino shrine|WD=Q135040486}} | イヒモチノ | Shikinai Shosha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182478 archive] | — | single | — | — | — |- | 1929 | 7 | Sawata district | {{ill|Woshikino shrine|WD=Q135040487}} | ヲシキノ | Shikinai Shosha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182479 archive] | — | single | — | — | — |- | 1930 | 8 | Kamo district | {{ill|Ohohatano shrine|WD=Q135040488}} | オホハタノ | Shikinai Shosha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182480 archive] | — | single | — | — | — |- | 1931 | 9 | Kamo district | {{ill|Atsukushihikono shrine|WD=Q135040490}} | アツクシヒコノ | Shikinai Shosha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182481 archive] | — | single | — | — | — |}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:08, 5 July 2025 (UTC)}} This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Sado Province''' or the list of shrines in [[Sado Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}
On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[Sado Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the Engishiki list. They are shown in a separate table to avoid confusion.
The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} counts 9 {{ill|Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the province – 9 {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} and 0 {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|WD=Q134917288}}.

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:54, 5 July 2025 (UTC)}} This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Sado Province''' or the list of shrines in [[Sado Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}
On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[Sado Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the Engishiki list. They are shown in a separate table to avoid confusion.
The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} counts 9 {{ill|Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the province – 9 {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} and 0 {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|WD=Q134917288}}.

On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[Sado Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the Engishiki list. They are shown in a separate table to avoid confusion.
The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} counts 9 {{ill|Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the province – 9 {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} and 0 {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|WD=Q134917288}}.

This is a list of Shikinaisha in [[Sado Province]]. That is the shrines in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] in the province.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 9 Shikinaisha in the province – 9 Shikinai Shosha and 0 Shikinai Taisha.

This is a list of Shikinaisha in [[Sado Province]]. That is the shrines in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] in the province.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 9 Shikinaisha in the province – 9 Shikinai Shosha and 0 Shikinai Taisha.

This is a list of Shikinaisha in [[Sado Province]]. That is the shrines in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] in the province.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 9 Shikinaisha in the province – 9 Shikinai Shosha and 0 Shikinai Taisha.
